name: Water Turret
notes: Rivergate and Kraken Point's shared specialty defense (renamed from River Battery), in addition to the generic Turret/Missile Launcher every base gets. A literal high-pressure water cannon — the jet is the weapon, no explosive/ballistic payload involved, which is why it trades Air targeting for a bonus against Naval targets instead: hard to aim a water jet skyward at a moving aircraft, but devastating against a hull at the waterline. At Rivergate it covers a river crossing (rivers are naval-passable all the way inland from the sea, so without this an enemy fleet could otherwise sail straight past uncontested); at Kraken Point it's the naval capstone base's coastal defense, reusing the same building rather than inventing a second one, same pattern as Granite Crumbler/Juggernaut sharing across bases. Must be placed adjacent to Water (Ocean or River) so it can actually cover the crossing/coastline it exists to defend. Multi-material like Tower/Wall/Dock/Bridge (materials/materialStats) rather than a Turret extends-variant, since each material plays genuinely differently, matching Tower's split: Stone is the balanced baseline. Wood is cheap/fragile/Fire-vulnerable but attacks fastest and is priced to spam early against small raiding fleets, at the cost of the smallest per-hit Naval bonus. Steel is the priciest, tankiest (flat armor, mirroring Steel Tower/Steel Wall), splashes on hit (like Steel Tower's bunker role) and carries the largest single-target Naval multiplier — a slow-firing coastal bunker built to punish a grouped push rather than chase individual raiders.
